members.name: Xingqi Wu
members.bio: Xingqi Wu is a Ph.D. researcher developing multi-agent reinforcement learning and large language model methods for resource orchestration, admission control, and resilience in 5G and 6G open radio access networks (O-RAN). His work spans network slicing, microservice provisioning across edge-cloud systems, and LLM-empowered xApps for adaptive radio resource management.

members.name: Yuhui Wang
members.bio: Yuhui Wang is a Ph.D. researcher and Rackham Predoctoral Fellow working on UAV communication networks. His research applies deep reinforcement learning to UAV placement, integrated access and backhaul, multi-UAV mobile edge computing, and UAV-assisted LEO satellite edge systems for resilient coverage and connectivity.

members.name: Elahe Delavari
members.bio: Elahe Delavari is a Ph.D. researcher focused on reinforcement-learning-driven control for open radio access networks. Her work includes jamming-resilient physical resource block (PRB) reservation and resilient network slicing for latency-critical industrial 5G systems.

alumni.name: Ritesh Honnalli
alumni.note: AI-driven cybersecurity for EV charging systems
